variant: summarize the key stats on sleep and muscle growth — bold the numbers

request.message: summarize the key stats on sleep and muscle growth — bold the numbers

response_body.response: sleep and muscle growth are locked together. here's what moves the needle.  the chain is simple: bad sleep tanks testosterone and GH, which kills the growth signal even if you're training hard. you need 7–9 hours consistently. if you're under 6, you're losing 10–15% test within a week. that's not a small thing. on the muscle side, you grow ~0.25–0.5 lb/week as an intermediate lifter if everything else is dialed. that means 10–20 hard sets per muscle per week, training each muscle 2–3 times, and staying within 0–3 reps of failure on your working sets. more volume past 20 sets doesn't help. protein synthesis stays elevated for 24–48 hours post-training, so spacing sessions out matters. the sleep part is non-negotiable. 70–80% of your daily GH comes during slow-wave sleep in the first 2 hours, so a cool dark room (65–68°f) and a 60-minute wind-down before bed actually move the needle.

products.name: Optimum Nutrition Gold Standard 100% Whey Protein
products.description: 24g whey isolate per scoop — third-party tested, low-cost per gram.
